repos.name: Zentrix Pulse
issues: `.env` with Supabase key is git-tracked; `.gitignore` only excludes `*.local`
TURN server credentials hardcoded in iceServers.ts (openrelayproject)
Metered API key exposed client-side via VITE_ prefix in .env.local

repos.name: DietForge
issues: `xlsx@0.18.5` has known CVE-2023-30533 (prototype pollution) — upgrade needed
Supabase anon key persists in git history despite .gitignore fix (rotate key)
`apiKeyUtils.ts:49` timing-attack vulnerable: non-constant-time `===` comparison

repos.name: Zentrix Insights
issues: .env with Supabase key committed to git history (commit b262028b) — rotate key
SSO tokens passed via URL hash (AuthContext:303) may leak through Referer headers
108 console.log calls across 20 files; vite-plugin-remove-console is devDep only

repos.name: Zentrix Mail
issues: .env committed with live Supabase JWT; sanitizeSqlString() is bypassable
CSP allows 'unsafe-inline'+'unsafe-eval', defeating XSS protection
Custom HTML sanitizer via dangerouslySetInnerHTML; no DOMPurify

repos.name: Lineage Vault
issues: `.env` with Supabase keys not in `.gitignore` — tracked in repo
`innerHTML` assignment in BucketListMap.tsx — potential XSS if data is untrusted
`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in chart.tsx without visible sanitization

repos.name: Zentrix Cyber
issues: `.env` committed to git history (bed74f4) — rotate Supabase keys
`VITE_ZENTRIX_API_KEY` exposed client-side; secret keys must stay server-side
Client-side role insert on signup — attacker can escalate via RLS bypass

repos.name: Zentrix Projects
issues: `.env` with Supabase credentials tracked in git history (even if gitignored now)
`xlsx` v0.18.5 uses SheetJS CE — verify no prototype pollution CVEs apply
No CSP headers configured; relies entirely on Supabase RLS for authorization

repos.name: Decision Command
issues: `.env` with Supabase anon key committed to git (not gitignored)
Verbose console.log throughout src/ may leak user IDs and decision data
No RLS bypass risk but anon key exposure widens attack surface

repos.name: Improvements Dashboard
issues: No deps listed in package.json; no lockfile for reproducible builds
Static JSON data served without cache/integrity headers (vercel.json)
Shell scripts in scripts/ may need input sanitization (unverified)

repos.name: WiseVAs
issues: `.env` has hardcoded Sentry DSN; Supabase keys missing but DSN committed
`candidates/${id}` path interpolation lacks input sanitization
`.env` gitignored but file exists with real credentials in working tree

repos.name: Zentrix CRM
issues: `.env` has real Supabase anon key committed to repo (check git history)
`xlsx` 0.18.5 has known prototype pollution CVE (CVE-2024-22363)
`ActivityDetailModal` uses `convertTextToHtml` in dangerouslySetInnerHTML unsanitized

repos.name: Zentrix Creative
issues: `.env` with Supabase credentials is committed to git history
`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in chart.tsx — low risk but review for XSS
Min password length only 6 chars — weak for production auth policy

repos.name: Zentrix OS
issues: debugAuth.ts logs userId + session metadata; verify prod logger suppresses
stripe-config.ts hardcodes pk_live key in source (public by design, but noisy)
No significant injection or auth bypass risks found in scanned files

repos.name: Thoughtline
issues: `.env` with Supabase keys persists in git history (commits fb98cd4, 7b5ccce)
Edge functions (scrape-instagram, enhance-thought) lack JWT auth checks
User content interpolated unsanitized into LLM prompt (prompt injection)

repos.name: Zentrix Marketing
issues: .env has real Supabase anon key + project ID committed to repo
dangerouslySetInnerHTML in chart.tsx — potential XSS if data is untrusted
No significant auth/injection/CVE issues found beyond the above

repos.name: Zentrix Capture
issues: Billing edge function CORS allows only localhost; prod domain missing (TODO)
Anonymous uploads allowed without rate limiting — abuse vector
View counter in Watch.tsx does unauthenticated DB update — inflatable

repos.name: Zentrix Sign
issues: `.env` with live Supabase credentials on disk (not committed, but no .env.example)
SignDocument page queries signers table with anon key — relies solely on RLS
No rate limiting on public signing endpoint `/sign/:token`

repos.name: Zentrix Chat
issues: `.env` with Supabase keys is tracked in git (committed in repo history)
Webhook secrets fetched client-side via RPC — secrets exposed to browser
`.gitignore` won't prevent `.env` leak; file already committed to history

repos.name: Zentrix Email Mktg
issues: Supabase credentials hardcoded in .env committed to source control
5 vulnerable deps (picomatch CVE, esbuild SSRF, yaml stack overflow)
Auth sessions stored in localStorage, vulnerable to XSS token theft

repos.name: Zentrix Content
issues: `.env` with Supabase anon key is committed to git (tracked in repo history)
API key revocation bypasses RLS via client-side `as any` cast on table
No `.env.example`; real credentials ship as defaults for all clones

repos.name: Zentrix Finance
issues: `.env` tracked in git & not in `.gitignore` — Supabase keys exposed in repo
No RLS bypass risk but missing `.gitignore` for `.env*` files is a leak vector
`dangerouslySetInnerHTML` in chart.tsx — safe here (static config) but unaudited
